message:
fix(scheduler): collapse collision slots when task generations drain

The ScheduledSet uses a dense array for O(1) membership checks, with an
overflow HashSet for the rare case where two generations of the same arena
index coexist. When a collision was detected, the dense slot was marked
with DENSE_COLLISION and both entries moved to overflow. However, when
those colliding generations were later removed, the dense slot was never
restored—it stayed permanently in collision mode, forcing all future
generations of that index through the slower overflow path.

Add `collapse_collision_slot()` that is called after every removal from
overflow. It checks how many generations remain for the given arena index:

- Zero remaining: reset dense slot to 0 (empty)
- One remaining: move the sole survivor back to dense storage and remove
  it from overflow, restoring the fast path
- Two or more remaining: keep the collision marker (no change)

This prevents gradual performance degradation in long-running runtimes
where task IDs are recycled across many generations.

- Add test `scheduled_set_collision_slot_clears_when_generations_drain`
  verifying the slot resets to 0 after both colliding generations are
  removed, and a new generation uses dense storage directly
- Add test `scheduled_set_collision_slot_collapses_to_single_remaining_generation`
  verifying the slot collapses to dense tracking when one of two colliding
  generations is removed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
